title: izba dogfood journeys (Phase 1 output / Phase 2 input)
description: A feature's user journeys: goals a real user would have given the spec's promises, decomposed into ordered intent+expectation steps. Produced by the local Phase 1 (intent extraction) harness; consumed by the Phase 2 (journey execution) runner.

feature.description: Human-readable name of the feature under test.

journey_id.description: Stable, unique-within-feature kebab-case id. Used for sharding and loop-dedup.

rationale.description: Why this journey exists: the spec promise / expectation it probes.

modality.description: Action surface this journey runs against. 'cli' (default) = izba shell commands via run_journeys.py; 'gui' = the Tauri app driven through a browser via run_gui_journeys.py. Absent ⇒ treated as 'cli'.

tier.description: Progressive-exploration depth (Phase 2 sequencing). 'smoke' = shallow happy-path + capability probes run first and cheap; 'core' = main feature coverage; 'deep' = adversarial / edge / multi-step. Deeper tiers are dispatched only after the prior tier's gating journeys established the prerequisite capabilities (post in-place fix). Optional; absent ⇒ treated as 'core'.

establishes.description: Capability ids this journey proves usable when it genuinely succeeds (e.g. 'install-tooling-under-enforce', 'tls-verifies-under-enforce'). Smoke journeys establish the prerequisites deeper journeys depend on. Optional; kebab-case capability tokens shared across journeys of one feature.

requires.description: Capability ids that must be established (by an earlier-tier journey, possibly after an in-place fix) before this journey is worth running. If a required capability is a confirmed unfixable blocker, the sequencer/orchestrator defers this journey and records why (never silently). Optional.

gating.description: When true, this journey is a gate for its tier: advancing to the next tier requires it to genuinely succeed (or its blocker to be fixed in-place). Use sparingly — only for the capability prerequisites deeper tiers truly depend on. Optional; default false.

seed_files.description: Precondition files materialized into the journey's workdir (HOST-side — the dir the Actor's shell runs in on the machine running izba, never inside a guest) BEFORE its first step: a map of relative path -> file content. Models an environment precondition a deep journey legitimately assumes (the same contract 'establishes'/'requires' encode), NOT a hint to the Actor: seed content lives here in journeys.json, which the Actor never sees. Traversal-guarded (relative paths only, no '..' escape). Compiler rule: seed a precondition, NEVER the thing under test (e.g. seeding a valid izba.yml for a review-gate journey is a precondition; seeding it for a manifest-authoring journey would destroy the test). Optional.

kind.description: Where the expectation was traced from (spec | pr | greptile review | --help topic | README).

ref.description: Pointer into the source (doc + section, PR number, review id, help topic).

intent.description: What the user wants to do at this step (intent, NOT a literal command). The Actor chooses the concrete izba invocation.

expect.description: The expected observable outcome of this step, in user-observable terms (judged by the Phase-3 trajectory-skeptic against product state — NOT a literal exit-code assertion).

core.description: Marks this step as DECISIVE: the functional assertion graded here governs the journey's positive/negative tally (setup/recovery noise in non-core steps is downgraded to soft). If no step in a journey is marked core, the LAST step is treated as decisive. Optional; default false.

expect_exit.description: Declarative exit-code assertion that supersedes the fragile 'expect' keyword heuristic when present: an integer N grades the step's final action as PASS iff it exited exactly N; the string 'nonzero' grades PASS iff it exited non-zero (the way to assert an expected refusal, e.g. `izba ssh NAME -- false`). When absent, the 'expect' English-keyword path is the fallback (backward compatible). When the step also declares expect_cmd_re, the graded action is the last one matching that regex instead of the final action. Optional.

expect_cmd_re.description: Optional regex anchoring WHICH of the step's actions the functional/expect_exit verdict is graded against: the LAST action whose command matches. Without it the step's final action is graded — which false-fires when the Actor runs a trailing verify (e.g. `izba ls`) after the intent-bearing command. Compiler rule: set it on refusal/expect_exit steps to the distinctive token of the command under test (e.g. 'promote'); never anchor to an exact full command line (that would leak a prescription to the swarm's benefit — the regex lives here, invisible to the Actor). Decisive (core) steps SHOULD declare expect_cmd_re: it also lets the runner credit a decisive assertion satisfied under an earlier step instead of flagging unreached_decisive.
